comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v3.2/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
I don't know if this is a candidate for RHEL 8.4.0.2, Podman 3.2.3, but I'm leery about merging this one in, especially without man page changes, just before we release....
Why would you want man page changes, I believe this was breaking a previous non libimage version of Podman.  Basically auto-update was attempting to pull images from localhost/image.

Unless I'm completely misunderstanding the change, this changes the way --pull works dependent upon the image that's being requested.

http://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-build.1.html#pull

Doesn't mention anything about what happens when `localhost` is part of the value passed into the image.

I hope to find time next week to revisit a bit all the multi-arch issues and write to down exactly what happens when.  That will be a good exercise to find some improvements but also to massage that into the man pages.

FWIW, nothing has changed in Podman.  `podman pull` has always enforced --pull=always when custom arch, os or variant were specified.  Buildah behaved slightly different prior to libimage.
